name: Crawfish of Shrimp Etouffee
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the seasoning mix ingredients and set aside.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the chopped onions, celery, and green peppers.
3. Heat the oil in a large skillet (preferably cast iron) over high heat until it begins to smoke. This should take about 4 minutes.
4. Gradually mix in the flour using a long-handled whisk, stirring constantly until smooth.
5. Continue cooking the roux, whisking constantly, until it reaches a dark brown color. This should take about 3-5 minutes.
6. Remove the skillet from heat and stir in the vegetables and 1 tablespoon of the seasoning mix with a wooden spoon. Stir until cooled for about 5 minutes.
7. In a 2-quart saucepan, bring 2 cups of seafood stock to a boil over high heat. Gradually add the roux, whisking until thoroughly dissolved.
8. Reduce the heat to low and cook until the flour taste is gone, which should take about 2 minutes. Stir constantly.
9. Remove from heat and set aside.
10. In a 4-quart saucepan, melt 1 stick of butter over medium heat. Add the shrimp and green onions, stirring almost constantly for 1 minute.
11. Add the remaining stick of butter and stir in the stock mixture. Cook until the butter melts and is mixed into the sauce, which should take about 4-6 minutes. Shake the pan frequently.
12. Add the remaining seasoning mix and stir well. If the sauce separates, add about 2 tablespoons of water or stock and shake the pan until it combines.
13. Serve immediately.
